analysis: Broad and strict HbA1c cis-instrument MR across biliary endpoints
contribution: Initial genetic evidence for GIPR-region association with biliary outcomes
interpretation_if_supportive: Supports a GIPR-region locus-proxy association, especially if strict-window estimates are coherent
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Keeps inference at exploratory locus-proxy level; does not support target-level claim

analysis: Broad ±1 Mb versus strict ±200 kb GIPR comparison
contribution: Tests whether regional pleiotropy affects interpretability
interpretation_if_supportive: Strict-window consistency suggests less regional contamination
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Broad-only signal would be considered unstable

analysis: Wald ratio comparison for rs10407429 and rs17561351
contribution: Shows variant-level disagreement within broad-window GIPR proxy
interpretation_if_supportive: Divergent variant estimates support need for strict-window sensitivity
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Concordant estimates would weaken the pleiotropy-contamination explanation

analysis: PheWAS at p<1e-5 and domain summary
contribution: Characterizes non-target association burden in the two GIPR-region variants
interpretation_if_supportive: rs17561351 enrichment for lipid/platelet domains supports competing-regional-biology interpretation
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Absence of domain differences would weaken the regional pleiotropy interpretation
manuscript_use: Source Data 15 and variant-trait association-map Results

analysis: Directional map for glycaemic, lipid, platelet, anthropometric, biliary, and pancreatic domains
contribution: Displays competing glycaemic versus lipid/platelet patterns between retained and excluded variants
interpretation_if_supportive: Retained variant aligns more with glycaemic traits while excluded variant carries lipid/platelet burden
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Mixed patterns require conservative interpretation and no target-level claim

analysis: Positive glycaemic controls and negative non-biliary controls
contribution: Tests whether expected glycaemic signals are present and distant outcomes are not broadly positive
interpretation_if_supportive: Positive controls support proxy validity; negative controls reduce concern about nonspecific procedure/disease association
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: Unexpected negative-control signals would weaken specificity and require additional caution

analysis: Targeted scan for GIPR cis-pQTL/eQTL evidence
contribution: Assesses whether the HbA1c proxy can be tied directly to GIPR molecular regulation
interpretation_if_supportive: Direct GIPR QTL evidence would strengthen target-biology interpretation
interpretation_if_null_or_infeasible: No direct cached GIPR QTL support keeps inference at GIPR-region HbA1c cis-proxy level

analysis: Assessment of coloc-ready regional summary statistics
contribution: Determines whether shared-causal-variant claims can be tested
interpretation_if_supportive: High-quality coloc would support shared signal language

analysis: Comparison with incretin therapy safety literature and weight-loss context
contribution: Connects genetic evidence to clinical pharmacology without substituting for trials
interpretation_if_supportive: Alignment supports biological plausibility for biliary safety attention
